Definition: A unique ID for every Speedtest performed based on the platform.
A globally unique ID for every Speedtest performed.
Denotes the platform on which the test was taken:1 = Android2 = iOS
Date and time of the test in UTC.
Date and time that Ookla received the test result from the device.
Name of user's timezone at location of test.
Public-facing unique ID of the user/device.

The device model and OS version of the user's device. Not standardized across all manufacturers. Rather than Android fingerprint, rely on the device and hardware fields for accurate information about the device hardware.
The Ookla-derived common name of the model of the device used for testing.
The manufacturer of the device used for testing.
Raw (or Android API-returned) model of the device.
Raw (or Android API-returned) manufacturer of the device.
Raw (or Android API-returned) brand of the device.
Ookla-maintained device chipset name.
Ookla-maintained device chipset manufacturer name.
The device hardware name, as reported by the Android kernel.
The version of the operating system being used by the device at the time of the test.

The Android build ID reflects the version of Android installed on the device. The build ID typically follows a standard convention of AAA##A, but some device manufacturers chose to implement their own convention. More information about Android build IDs can be found at https://source.android.com/source/build-numbers.html.
TRUE if the device allows running applications with root level permissions, otherwise FALSE. Note, if TRUE the device may be running software that reports false information.
The version string for the radio firmware.
Maximum system memory in megabytes available to UE kernel.
Maximum capacity in megabytes of UE file system, excluding any secondary storage such as SD cards.
TRUE if the device is designated as a world phone by Android.

Indicates if the usage of multiple SIM cards is supported by the device and by the carrier.0 = MULTISIM_ALLOWED if the device supports multiple SIMs 1 = MULTISIM_NOT_SUPPORTED_BY_HARDWARE if the device does not support multiple SIMs2 = MULTISIM_NOT_SUPPORTED_BY_CARRIER in the device supports multiple SIMs, but the functionality is restricted by the carrier

The number of logical modems currently configured to be activated. 0 = voice, sms, data is not supported 1 = single standby mode (single SIM functionality) 2 = dual standby mode (dual SIM functionality)3 = tri standby mode (tri SIM functionality)
The number of logical modem that can be potentially active simultaneously, in terms of hardware capability.
TRUE if the device is currently on a technology (e.g. UMTS or LTE) which can support voice and data simultaneously. This can change based on location or network condition.

TRUE if a cellular data connection is allowed in the device.Whether cellular data connection is allowed considers all factors below:User turned on data setting isDataEnabled().Carrier allows data to be on.Network policy.And possibly others.
TRUE if the current device is "data capable" over a radio on the device. "Data capable" means that this device supports packet-switched data connections over the telephony network.
TRUE mobile data roaming is enabled on the subscription.
TRUE if a ICC (Integrated Circuit Card or smart card) card is present.

Indicates the phone's service state: 0 = STATE_IN_SERVICE. Normal operation condition, the phone is registered with an operator either in home network or in roaming.1 = STATE_OUT_OF_SERVICE. Phone is not registered with any operator, the phone can be currently searching a new operator to register to, or not searching to registration at all, or registration is denied, or radio signal is not available.)2 = STATE_EMERGENCY_ONLY. The phone is registered and locked. Only emergency numbers are allowed.3 = STATE_POWER_OFF. Radio of telephony is explicitly powered off.

The current thermal status of the device. 0 = not under thermal throttling1 = light throttling where UX is not impacted2 = moderate throttling where UX is not largely impacted3 = severe throttling where UX is largely impacted4 = critical, platform has done everything to reduce power5 = emergency, key components in platform are shutting down due to thermal condition. Device functionalities will be limited.6 = shutdown needed immediately

An estimate of how much thermal headroom the device currently has before hitting severe throttling. Values range from 0.0 to 1.0, where 1.0 indicates severe throttling threshold. Values may exceed 1.0, but there is no implied mapping to specific thermal status levels beyond that point.
TRUE if the user granted the app phone state permissions.
TRUE if the user granted the app fine location permissions.
TRUE if the user granted the app coarse location permissions.
TRUE if the user granted the app background location permission.
TRUE if the app has WiFi State permission. This enables access to information about Wi-Fi networks

The Ookla-maintained common name of the SIM card provider. In the case of MVNOs, this will be the name of the carrier that owns the PLMN network. This will be the mobile carrier name displayed in the Speedtest Intelligence portal.
The raw name of the SIM card provider as reported by the device. This will sometimes show the name of the MVNO rather than the carrier that owns the PLMN network.
The mobile country code (MCC) of the SIM card operator.
The mobile network code (MNC) of the SIM card operator.
Mobile carrier name from alternate SIM card when using a dual SIM device.
The mobile country code (MCC) from alternate SIM card when using a dual SIM device.
The mobile network code (MNC) from alternate SIM card when using a dual SIM device.
The mobile network code (MCC) associated with the connected cell site during the test.
The mobile network code (MNC) associated with the connected cell site during the test.
The Ookla-maintained name of the network operator.
ISP common name associated with IP Address.
ISP name associated with IP Address. In some cases, this may report a secondary name of an ISP, for example ABC Fiber.
The device's Type Allocation Code (TAC) from the active SIM slot.

Indicates the state of the default SIM card (or active SIM for API 26 and above): 0 = SIM_STATE_UNKNOWN. Unknown. Signifies that the SIM is in transition between states. For example, when the user inputs the SIM pin under PIN_REQUIRED state, a query for sim status returns this state before turning to SIM_STATE_READY.1 = SIM_STATE_ABSENT. No SIM card is available in the device.2 = SIM_STATE_PIN_REQUIRED. Locked: requires the user's SIM PIN to unlock.3 = SIM_STATE_PUK_REQUIRED. Locked: requires the user's SIM PUK to unlock.4 = SIM_STATE_NETWORK_LOCKED. Locked: requires a network PIN to unlock.5 = SIM_STATE_READY. Ready.6 = SIM_STATE_NOT_READY. SIM Card is NOT READY.7 = SIM_STATE_PERM_DISABLED. SIM Card Error, permanently disabled.8 = SIM_STATE_CARD_IO_ERROR. SIM Card Error, present but faulty.9 = SIM_STATE_CARD_RESTRICTED. SIM Card restricted, present but not usable due to carrier restrictions.

The communication protocol (TCP or HTTP) used by the test. Whenever possible, the test makes a direct TCP connection with the testing server, which allows for greater testing accuracy, especially at higher speeds. In instances where the device is unable to establish a TCP connection with the test server on the appropriate port, the test falls back to using basic HTTP file transfers to determine the device's bandwidth.
Denotes if the test was conducted over IPv4 (4) or IPv6 (6).

A number representing the device's connection type as detected before the test begins. Please see the connection type table for an explanation of each type. Android connection type as detected before the test starts: 0 = Unknown,1 = Cell, 2 = Wi-Fi, 3 = GPRS, 4 = EDGE, 5 = UMTS, 6 = CDMA, 7 = EVDO0, 8 = EVDOA, 9 = OnexRTT, 10 = HSDPA, 11 = HSPA, 12 = IDEN, 13 = EHRPD, 14 = EVDOB, 15 = LTE, 16 = HSUPA, 17 = HSPAP, 18 = GSM, 19 = TDSCDMA, 20 = IWLAN, 21 = LTE-CA, 22 = Ethernet, 23 = Bluetooth, 24 = NR iOS connection type as detected before the test starts: 0 = unknown; 1 = cell, 2 = wifi; 3 = GPRS; 4 = Edge; 5 = WCDMA; 6 = HSDPA; 7 = HSUPA; 8 = CDMA1x; 9 = CDMAEVDORev0; 10 = CDMAEVDORevB; 11 = eHRPD; 12 = LTE; 13 = Ethernet; 14 = Bluetooth; 15 = HRPD; 16 = CDMAEVDORevA; 17 = NRNSA; 18 = NRSA

A number representing the device's connection type as detected at the end of the test. Please see the connection type table for an explanation of each type. Android connection type as detected at the end of the test: 0 = Unknown; 1 = Cell; 2 = Wi-Fi; 3 = GPRS; 4 = EDGE; 5 = UMTS; 6 = CDMA; 7 = EVDO0; 8 = EVDOA; 9 = OnexRTT; 10 = HSDPA; 11 = HSPA; 12 = IDEN; 13 = EHRPD; 14 = EVDOB; 15 = LTE; 16 = HSUPA; 17 = HSPAP; 18 = GSM; 19 = TDSCDMA; 20 = IWLAN; 21 = LTE-CA; 22 = Ethernet; 23 = Bluetooth; 24 = NR iOS connection type as detected at the end of the test: 0 = unknown; 1 = cell; 2 = wifi; 3 = GPRS; 4 = Edge; 5 = WCDMA; 6 = HSDPA; 7 = HSUPA; 8 = CDMA1x; 9 = CDMAEVDORev0; 10 = CDMAEVDORevB; 11 = eHRPD; 12 = LTE; 13 = Ethernet; 14 = Bluetooth; 15 = HRPD; 16 = CDMAEVDORevA; 17 = NRNSA; 18 = NRSA

The number of unsuccessful TCP connections between the client and the server. A connection may fail due to delayed, lost, or corrupted packets resulting in new connection attempts to successfully complete a download test. Multiple failed connections may be an indicator of adverse network conditions.
TRUE if carrier aggregation is in use.

New Radio state of the mobile data network. Returns one of the following: 1 = RESTRICTED. The device is camped on an LTE cell that supports E-UTRA-NR Dual Connectivity(EN-DC) but either the use of dual connectivity with NR(DCNR) is restricted or NR is not supported by the selected PLMN.2 = NOT_RESTRICTED. The device is camped on an LTE cell that supports E-UTRA-NR Dual Connectivity(EN-DC) and both the use of dual connectivity with NR(DCNR) is not restricted and NR is supported by the selected PLMN.3 = CONNECTED. The device is camped on an LTE cell that supports E-UTRA-NR Dual Connectivity(EN-DC) and also connected to at least one 5G cell as a secondary serving cell.0 = NONE. The device isn't camped on an LTE cell or the LTE cell doesn't support E-UTRA-NR Dual Connectivity(EN-DC).
Access Point Name (APN) is the name of a gateway between a GSM, GPRS, 3G or 4G mobile network and another computer network, frequently the public Internet.

User ISP/connection type (estimated). 0 = Unknown, 1 = Dialup, 2 = Cable/DSL, 3 = Corporate and 4=Cellular, 5 = Satellite. Results with a net_speed_id of 4 are excluded from all Fixed aggregates except for "Mobile Broadband".

TRUE when the cellular data access network technology is NR. Useful for indicating if the underlying cellular connection is NR while connected to WiFi. Indicates the active data bearer for 5G standalone implementations.

Indicates if a network type override occurred. Often associated with the network type icon displayed on the device.0 = no override1 = LTE with carrier aggregation2 = LTE advanced pro3 = NR NSA4 = NR NSA mmWave5 = NR advanced (NR network with data rate that is higher than the generic 5G rate such as mmWave, unique bands, or carrier aggregation)
Downstream bandwidth for the network (in Kbps). This only refers to the estimated first hop transport bandwidth.
Upstream bandwidth for the network (in Kbps). This only refers to the estimated first hop transport bandwidth.

The NAT64 prefix in use on the link, if any. The NAT64 gateway is a translator between IPv4 and IPv6 protocols, for which function it needs at least one IPv4 address and an IPv6 network segment comprising a 32-bit address space.
The latitude of the device conducting the test. If location type equals 2, this value is approximate.
The longitude of the device conducting the test. If location type equals 2, this value is approximate.
The latitude of the device conducting the test at the beginning of the test. If location type equals 2, this value is approximate.
The longitude of the device conducting the test at the beginning of the test. If location type equals 2, this value is approximate.

The method used to determine the device's location at the start of the test. Whenever possible, the speedtest.net mobile application uses the device's location services to determine an accurate latitude and longitude of the device. However, in an effort to maintain a quick and easy user experience, if the device's location services are either unavailable or unable to return an accurate location within a few seconds, we fall back to using third party Geo-IP location services. 1 = GPS / device location services, 2 = Geo-IP.

The method used to determine the device's location at the end of the test. Whenever possible, the speedtest.net mobile application uses the device's location services to determine an accurate latitude and longitude of the device. However, in an effort to maintain a quick and easy user experience, if the device's location services are either unavailable or unable to return an accurate location within a few seconds, we fall back to using third party Geo-IP location services. 1 = GPS / device location services, 2 = Geo-IP.
The accuracy of the location data collected from the device expressed in meters.
Indicates the time elapsed (in ms) from when the user's location information was collected and the test was taken.
The altitude (in meters) above the WGS 84 reference ellipsoid. NULL If this location does not have an altitude value.
Estimated vertical (altitude) accuracy of this location, in meters.
Indicates the speed that the device may have been moving during the test, measured in meters per second.
Full location name including city, county, region, and country, if available.
Unabbreviated location name; typically the city where the test is taken.
Type of locality where test is taken. This is typically an incorporated city or town political entity.
Country where test is taken.
Country code where test is taken.
Region name where test is taken. This is the first-order civil entity in a country level. Depending on the country, these are states, provinces, or territories, etc.
Region name where test is taken. This is the second-order civil entity in a country level. Depending on the country, these are county-level names. Not all countries display this information.
Region name where test is taken. This is the third-order civil entity in a country level. Depending on the country, these are the level under counties. Not all countries display this information.
Postal code for the test location. The postal code is that same as what is used to address mail within the country.
The number of packages sent to host server from device.
The number of packages received by the host server from device.
The percentage of packet loss measured.
Indicates whether the speedtest was able to complete before the maximum test duration. TRUE = Early stop occurredFALSE = Early stop did not occur. Full test duration or byte limit was hit
The minimum result of the latency portion of the test, measured in milliseconds.
The interquartile mean result of the latency portion of the test, measured in milliseconds.
The maximum result of the latency portion of the test, measured in milliseconds.
The result of the multi-server latency, based on server selection, measured in milliseconds.
The minimum latency value measured during the download portion of the test. Also referred to as “loaded latency”.
The interquartile mean latency value measured during the download portion of the test. Also referred to as “loaded latency”.
The maximum latency value measured during the download portion of the test. Also referred to as “loaded latency”.
The minimum latency value measured during the upload portion of the test. Also referred to as “loaded latency”.
The interquartile mean latency value measured during the upload portion of the test. Also referred to as “loaded latency”.
The maximum latency value measured during the upload portion of the test. Also referred to as “loaded latency”.
The number of hops taken on the traceroute from the client to server.
This is the IP address of the first hop of traceroute during a test.
The time taken for the packet to be received after the first hop in the traceroute, measured in milliseconds.
This is the IP address of the second hop of traceroute during a test.
The time taken for the packet to be received after the second hop in the traceroute.
The variance in latency over time which determines the stability of the internet connection.
The result of the multi-server jitter, based on server selection, measured in milliseconds.
The result of the download portion of the test, measured in kilobits per second.
The bytes used during a test for the download portion, in kilobytes.
The maximum number of download threads used during adaptive testing, maximum of 22 threads
The length, in milliseconds, of the speedtest based on adaptive testing, maximum test length being 15 seconds
The result of the upload portion of the test, measured in kilobits per second.
The bytes used during a test for the upload portion, in kilobytes.
The maximum number of upload threads used during testing.
The length, in milliseconds, of the upload phase of the test.

This is the IPv4 address from which the request to Ookla's reporting API was generated. The last octet of the user's external IP address is masked for addresses that are not on your network. Full IP addresses for your network can be made available upon request and delivered in a separate supplemental dataset. Devices behind networks with carrier grade NAT may appear to share the same IP address.

Speedtest user's IPv6 address. We mask IP addresses for end users privacy, but show enough to allow clients to see which provider is offering the user's internet service.  Please note that this address may not be the connection that the Speedtest was taken over (unless there is no IPv4 address populated).
The Autonomous System Number (ASN) associated with the client's IP address.
Speedtest app version used for the test.
App store that app was downloaded from, such as the Amazon android app store, Google Play, Yandex, Samsung app store, etc.
The name of the server used for the test. This is typically the city where the server is located.
The name of the test server sponsor. Combining server name and server sponsor will create a unique name for each server.
The latitude of the server used for the test.
The longitude of the server used for the test.
The distance (in kilometers) between user and location of server.
The country where the server used for the test is located.
The two letter country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) of where the test server is located.

Whether the server used in the Speedtest was selected automatically or manually by the user  "TRUE" indicates that the server was selected automatically "FALSE" indicates that the server was selected manually by the user
TRUE if the test was taken on a server sponsored by the network operator.
The Autonomous System Number (ASN) associated with the primary server's IP.
The number of servers utilized during the download portion of the test.
Reference Signal Received Power LTE metric displaying the received power of the reference LTE signal, often referred to as "signal strength".  Range: [-40, -140], where -140 is poor and -44 is best

CSI Reference Signal Received Power (CSI-RSRP) The linear average over the power contributions (in [W]) of the resource elements of the antenna port(s) that carry CSI reference signals configured for RSRP measurements within the considered measurement frequency bandwidth in the configured CSI-RS occasions. Reference: 3GPP TS 38.215. Range: [-40, -140], where -140 is poor and -44 is best

Synchronization Signal Reference Signal Received Power (SS-RSRP) The linear average over the power contributions (in [W]) of the resource elements that carry secondary synchronization signals. Reference: 3GPP TS 38.215. [-40, -140], where -140 is poor and -44 is best
Reference Signal Received Quality The received quality of the LTE reference signal. Range: [-20, -3] where -20 is poor and -3 is best

CSI Reference Signal Received Quality (CSI-RSRQ) The ratio of N×CSI-RSRP to CSI-RSSI, where N is the number of resource blocks in the CSI-RSSI measurement bandwidth. Reference: 3GPP TS 38.215. Range: [-20, -3] where -20 is poor and -3 is best

Synchronization Signal Reference Signal Received Quality (SS-RSRQ) The ratio of N×SS-RSRP / NR carrier RSSI, where N is the number of resource blocks in the NR carrier RSSI measurement bandwidth. Reference: 3GPP TS 38.215. Range: [-20, -3] where -20 is poor and -3 is best
Reference Signal Signal-to-Noise Ratio It's the ratio between the noise and signal of the LTE data transmission. Range: [-30, +30], where -30 is poor and +30 is best

CSI signal-to-noise and interference ratio (CSI-SNR) The linear average over the power contribution (in [W]) of the resource elements carrying CSI reference signals divided by the linear average of the noise and interference power contribution (in [W]). Reference: 3GPP TS 38.215. Range: [-30, +30], where -30 is poor and +30 is best

SS signal-to-noise and interference ratio (SS-SNR) The linear average over the power contribution (in [W]) of the resource elements carrying secondary synchronisation signals divided by the linear average of the noise and interference power contribution (in [W]). Reference: 3GPP TS 38.215. Range: [-30, +30], where -30 is poor and +30 is best
Energy per chip over the noise spectral density (Ec/No) as dB.  Range: [-24, 1]
Received Strength Signal Indicator (non-LTE).
Received Signal Strength Indicator Range: [-113, -51]
LTE Timing Advance measured in Ts.

CQI stands for Channel Quality Indicator. As the name implies, it is an indicator carrying the information on how good/bad the communication channel quality is.  In LTE, there are 15 different CQI values ranging from 1 to 15 and mapping between CQI and modulcation scheme. In HSDPA, the CQI value ranges from 0 to 30. 30 indicates the best channel quality and 0,1 indicates the poorest channel quality.

The frequency range of 5G NR. 0 = FREQUENCY_RANGE_UNKNOWN. Indicates frequency range is unknown.1 = FREQUENCY_RANGE_LOW. Indicates the frequency range is below 1GHz.2 = FREQUENCY_RANGE_MID. Indicates the frequency range is between 1GHz to 3GHz.3 = FREQUENCY_RANGE_HIGH. Indicates the frequency range is between 3GHz and 6GHz.4 = FREQUENCY_RANGE_MMWAVE. Indicates the frequency range is above 6GHz.
Bandwidth of the primary serving cell, measured in kHz.
An array (JSON string containing an array of integers) of cell bandwidths (in kHz) for the current serving cells.
The primary cell ID that the device is connected to during the test.
The eNodeB ID of the cell site that the device is connected to during the test.

LTE Physical Cell Identity. An integer to identify the physical LTE cell the user is connected to. The value is unique to the physical cell antennae rather than a specific cell tower. Valid values are 0 to 503.

New Radio Physical Cell Identity. An integer to identify the physical nr cell the user is connected to. The value is unique to the physical cell antennae rather than a specific cell tower. Valid values are 0 to 1007.
Tracking Area Code. A 16 bit integer used to facilitate handoff of a device between cells. The Tracking Area Identity can be determined by prepending the MCC and MNC to the Tracking Area Code.
GSM, WCDMA. A 16-bit integer representing a cell's location within a given operator's system.
Primary Scrambling Code, applies to GSM and WCDMA only.  Range: [0, 512]

The Absolute Radio Frequency Channel Number (ARFCN) is a unique number given to each radio channel in GSM. The ARFCN can be used to calculate the exact frequency of the radio channel. Within the GSM900 band ARFCN 1 to 124 are used. In the GSM1800 band ARFCN 512 to 885 are used. The UTRA Absolute Radio Frequency Channel Number (UARFCN) is a unique number given to each radio channel within the frequency bands used by the UMTS UTRA. The UARFCN can be used to calculate the carrier frequency. The E-UTRA Absolute Radio Frequency Channel Number (EARFCN). In LTE, the carrier frequency in the uplink and downlink is designated by EARFCN, which ranges between 0-65535. EARFCN uniquely identify the LTE band and carrier frequency. EARFCN is independent of channel bandwidth.
The type of cell frequency channel, such as ARFCN, UARFCN, EARFCN.
New Radio Absolute Radio Frequency Channel Number. Reference: 3GPP TS 38.101-1 section 5.4.2.1 NR-ARFCN and channel raster. Reference: 3GPP TS 38.101-2 section 5.4.2.1 NR-ARFCN and channel raster.
An array of band numbers or empty array if not available.
An array of band numbers or empty array if not available.
TRUE if the device is roaming.
TRUE if the device is roaming internationally.
TRUE if the device was connected to a VPN.
TRUE if the device is 5G-capable.
TRUE if the test is included in the Speedtest Intelligence portal. FALSE if the test has been excluded from the Speedtest Intelligence portal.
A comma-separated list of Speedtest Intelligence portal aggregates where the test is included. Example: 4G LTE, 5G, Modern Chipsets
The connection type in string format at the test start.
The connection type in string format at the test end.
TRUE if the device has an eSIM embedded.
New Radio Cell Identity.
The starting ID of either the GSM or CDMA ID. It is useful to compare this to cdma_cell_id or gsm_cell_id to determine if the cell id changed during testing.
Mobile Country Code of New Radio cell.
Mobile Network Code of New Radio cell.
The unique Building ID where the measurement was estimated to have been captured. If NULL then the measurement was not tagged as being captured indoor.
The percentage (0% - 100%) level of likelihood that the measurement was captured indoor. If NULL then the measurement was not tagged as being captured indoor.
The device software version
The signal cell type.
The traceroute 0 ping.
The traceroute 1 ping.
The Cell GSM ID

ASU Level - Arbitrary Strength UnitTypically it is a value between 0 and 97 with with 99 indicating unknownmobile signal strength measured by the android phone forcellular network type—typically GSM/UMTS/LTE.
NR ASU Level = Arbitrary Strength UnitTypically it is a value between 0 and 97 with with 99 indicating unknownmobile signal strength measured by the android phone fornetwork type - NR 5G)
